item.title: Medicinal Plants and the Gastrointestinal Microbiota in Chronic Diseases Modulation: A Structured Mechanistic and Translational Review.

item.title: Intestinal epithelial Syndecan-1 maintains mucosal homeostasis in inflammatory bowel disease by enhancing Faecalibacterium prausnitzii biofilm formation.
item.description: Gut microbes - Despite the rising global incidence of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), curative therapies remain unavailable. While our previous work implicated the intestinal proteoglycan Syndecan-1 (SDC1) in I...

item.title: Bioengineering a probiotic to bloom during colonic inflammation promotes reliable efficacy in translational models of colitis.
item.description: Gastroenterology - Emerging insights into the gut microbiome have sparked interest in exploring microbial therapeutics for treating inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD). However, no microbial therapeutics have yet shown...

item.title: Microbiota-derived metabolites and cardiovascular implications in Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD).
item.description: Molecular medicine (Cambridge, Mass.) - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, is a chronic relapsing systemic disorder associated with significant extraintestinal manifestations. Emerging ...

item.title: Early gut-vascular barrier breakdown precedes colitis onset in murine models.

item.title: BACH2 controls ILC3 function via PPARγ-dependent mitochondrial metabolism.

item.title: Medicinal and Edible Plants for the Management of Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Synergistic Modulation of the Microbiota-Metabolite-Immune Axis.

item.title: Intestinal microenvironment-modulating nanomedicines for inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of controlled release : official journal of the Controlled Release Society - Review - Inflammatory bowel diseases present a highly restrictive intestinal microenvironment for drug delivery, in which epithelial injury, chronic inflammation, and microbial dysbiosis collectively impair...

item.title: Interaction between Chinese olive (Canarium album L.) polyphenols and gut microbiota: Potential implications for inflammatory bowel disease.

item.title: The Overlap Between Crohn's Disease and Intestinal Tuberculosis: A Never-Ending Story.
item.description: Medicina (Kaunas, Lithuania) - Review - The prevalence of Crohn's disease has increased over the last few decades, even in developing countries, whereas that of intestinal tuberculosis has decreased, which places both diseases at an epid...

item.title: Oral cascade-responsive nanomedicine to disrupt macrophage-neutrophil crosstalk for intestinal barrier repair.

item.title: Laetiporus sulphureus polysaccharides mitigate colitis by reshaping the gut microbiota and regulating immune responses.
item.description: Frontiers in pharmacology - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) involves epithelial barrier disruption, immune dysregulation, and microbial imbalance. The present study investigated the protective mechanisms of Laetiporus sulphu...

item.title: Overexpression of mitochondrial STAT3 protein improves colonic inflammation and fibrosis in inflammatory bowel disease by enhancing mitochondrial function.

item.title: Purinergic Signaling, HIF, and ROS Interactions in Myocardial I/R Injury: Therapeutic Potential and Future Prospective.
